game_name: M.U.S.H.A.: Metallic Uniframe Super Hybrid Armor

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player begins with unpowered main gun; core mechanic of every engagement
Collect to upgrade main gun; three chips accumulate into drone reward

element_introduction_order.element: Special weapons (piercing laser, fire explosives, rotating shield)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Pickup-based weapons that trade survivability: equipped special weapon loss vs. life loss when hit
Earned via Power Chips (every 3 collected); six configurable attack modes; max 2 equipped, extras stored
Enemies move in/out of screen depth; escalates across stages
Tiles fall into screen; requires spatial awareness and dodging
Stage-ending enemies requiring learned attack patterns; escalating difficulty

element_introduction_order.element: Parallax scrolling depth (valley stage technical showcase)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Visual reinforcement of enemy/object depth via Doppler effect sound modulation

typical_relief_triggers: Power Chip collection (upgrades sense of progression)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Speed metal soundtrack matches fast vertical scrolling action; composed to build intensity through heavy metal rhapsody structure rather than fusion/mechanical soundscapes

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of core mechanics (main gun, Power Chips, special weapons, drones) with manageable enemy density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation of enemy wave patterns and introduction of environmental hazards; parallax depth stage increases spatial complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters demand learned attack pattern recognition; enemy density and speed intensify
notable_spikes: Valley stage parallax scrolling (technical/visual difficulty spike)

notable_level_patterns.description: Mid-game stage utilizing Genesis vertical parallax scrolling with enemies moving in/out of screen depth; Doppler effect sound modulation enhances spatial perception
Each stage concludes with boss encounter featuring distinct visual identity (e.g., character with Noh mask) and pattern-based attack sequences
Falling tiles programmed to move in/out of screen depth; requires continuous spatial awareness during enemy engagement
